ctx.name: Carl
ctx.notes: They have been doing well but letting the CoE go limp

introQueue.label: Anything to cover
introQueue.name: Before we start, is there anything specific you want to make sure we cover today?
introQueue.description: Manager-led check that the report has agenda input before the prepared questions begin.

introQueue.label: Agenda check
introQueue.name: Before we get into it, anything you want to make sure we cover today?
introQueue.description: Semi-set early question. Gives the report explicit permission to set the agenda before the manager's plan takes over.

introQueue.label: Friction scan
introQueue.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
introQueue.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

introQueue.label: Opening pace check
introQueue.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
introQueue.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

focus_points.label: Keeping the CoE moving with clear bets
focus_points.reason: Notes say they’ve been doing well but letting the CoE go limp. One possibility is the CoE’s priorities and momentum aren’t being actively sequenced or protected—worth clarifying what’s slipped and what they think the CoE should be driving next.

focus_points.label: How they’re keeping partners aligned to CoE work
focus_points.reason: If the CoE is losing momentum, it often shows up as weaker pull from product/design partners or unclear expectations. How they’re managing those relationships could be the difference between a CoE that’s used vs one that fades.

focus_points.label: Delegating CoE ownership without losing momentum
focus_points.reason: Whether they’re still driving the CoE directly or quietly letting others carry it without enough coaching and follow-through. At Lead level, CoE momentum depends on delegation with real ownership and visible outcomes.

focus_points.label: CoE updates that create pull, not noise
focus_points.reason: Whether their CoE communication is making it easy for others to know what’s happening, what decisions are needed, and how to engage. Leads often lose momentum when updates become status-only instead of decision- and action-oriented.

brief.coreIssue: This check-in is likely about how Carl is exercising lead-level ownership to restore momentum and direction in the CoE.
brief.openingQuestion: What would keeping the CoE meaningfully moving over the next few weeks look like from your side, and where is it getting stuck?
listenFor: whether he names one or two concrete CoE bets instead of speaking in broad themes
whether he points to a specific partner or forum where alignment on CoE work is slipping
if they describe who owns each CoE thread now or stay vague about delegated ownership
avoid: do not turn this into a critique of everything the CoE has not delivered
do not jump straight into prescribing priorities before you hear how Carl sees the CoE's role and current drag
brief.goodOutcome: You and Carl have agreed one lead-level CoE priority he will actively steer end-to-end, with clear ownership boundaries for what he delegates versus drives himself.
brief.suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where the CoE lost momentum or visibility so you can test specifics instead of speaking in general impressions.

issues: listenFor may lack observable behavioural cue: "if they describe who owns each CoE thread now or stay vague …"
